title: An overview of diarrhea in ICU patients receiving enteral nutrition
theme_label: Review or overview with no explicit fiber
paper_note: Review-style ICU diarrhea paper had no explicit fiber intervention, so exclusion was defensible.
representative_rationale: Title/abstract describe a general overview of enteral-nutrition–related diarrhea in ICU patients and do not indicate any dietary fiber-containing enteral intervention.

title: Diarrhea in intensive care: Diagnosis and treatment
theme_label: Review or overview with no explicit fiber
paper_note: Another generic ICU diarrhea review that humans sent to full text but models saw as clearly out of scope.
representative_rationale: ICU population is relevant, but the title/abstract do not describe a dietary-fiber enteral intervention/exposure; they discuss diarrhea diagnosis/treatment and generic enteral nutrition.

title: The effects of psyllium hydrophilic mucilloid on diarrhea in enterally fed patients
paper_note: The full text described a mixed ICU and medical-surgical sample, so strict exclusion was understandable.
representative_rationale: Full text states subjects were enrolled from both ICU and general medical-surgical units, so the population was not 100% ICU/critically ill as required.

title: Benefits of a synbiotic formula (Synbiotic 2000 Forte) in critically Ill trauma patients: early results of a randomized controlled trial
paper_note: Comparator eligibility in the synbiotic trauma trial appears genuinely contestable from the criteria wording.
representative_rationale: Peer-reviewed English RCT in 100% ICU/critically ill trauma patients testing enterally administered synbiotic formula containing prebiotic fiber components versus non-fiber placebo, with clinical health outcomes reported.

title: Additional oligofructose/inulin does not increase faecal bifidobacteria in critically ill patients receiving enteral nutrition: a randomised controlled trial
paper_note: The placebo was non-fiber, but the formula context made the comparator logic debatable.
representative_rationale: Peer-reviewed English RCT in 100% ICU adults receiving enteral fiber-containing formula plus oligofructose/inulin versus non-fiber placebo, reporting clinical/biological outcomes.

title: Effect of a fecal bulking agent on diarrhea during enteral feeding in the critically ill
paper_note: The comparator looked fiber-based to one reading and non-fiber enough to another.
representative_rationale: Randomized ICU trial of enteral ispaghula husk, but the comparator is a wheat-based breakfast cereal placebo, i.e., a fiber-based placebo/comparator, which is excluded for RCTs.

title: The effects of synbiotics on morbidity and mortality in newborns with cyanotic congenital heart disease: A prospective randomized controlled trial
paper_note: Neonatal/NICU population was outside the intended adult critical-care target.
representative_rationale: NICU newborns received an enteral synbiotic (prebiotic/probiotic) in an RCT; placebo type is not specified, so there is no clear L1 exclusion.

title: The propre-save study: effects of probiotics and prebiotics alone or combined on necrotizing enterocolitis in very low birth weight infants
paper_note: Very-low-birth-weight infant study was treated as on-target ICU evidence.
representative_rationale: NICU infants received enteral prebiotic/probiotic/synbiotic in an RCT, matching ICU population and eligible fiber-related exposure.

title: Tolerance, safety, and effect on the faecal microbiota of an enteral formula supplemented with pre- and probiotics in critically ill children
paper_note: Pediatric ICU synbiotic trial was advanced despite the review targeting adult critical-care patients.
representative_rationale: ICU/critically ill children received an enteral synbiotic formula containing fiber components; comparator details are not fully clear, so this should advance to L2.

title: Soy-polysaccharide fiber: Effect on diarrhea in tube-fed, head-injured patients
theme_label: Non icu or mixed population boundary
paper_note: Head-injured tube-fed patients were treated as ICU/critical-care despite no explicit ICU signal.
representative_rationale: Fiber-containing enteral RCT is relevant, but the abstract does not explicitly confirm all patients were ICU/critically ill, so it should be retained for L2.

title: Fiber-fortified feedings in immobile patients
theme_label: Non icu or mixed population boundary
paper_note: Immobile tube-fed patients were advanced on topic similarity alone, not population match.
representative_rationale: Fiber-fortified enteral feeding is relevant, but the abstract does not clearly state whether all patients were ICU/critically ill, so population remains unclear at L1.

title: Reduction in diarrhea incidence by soluble fiber in patients receiving total or supplemental enteral nutrition
theme_label: Generic enteral fiber relevance without clear icu match
paper_note: Postoperative enteral-nutrition trial lacked the ICU/critical-ill population required by the review.
representative_rationale: Fiber-supplemented enteral RCT is on topic, but the abstract does not explicitly confirm a 100% ICU/critically ill population, so retain for L2.
